sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, data VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, created_at) VALUES (1, 'abcdef', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 00:00:00'), (2, 'ghijkl', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-02 00:00:00'), (3, NULL, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-03 00:00:00');
SELECT reverse(data) AS reversed_data, created_at - INTERVAL 1 DAY AS new_created_at FROM test_table WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL;
SELECT a.id, reverse(b.data) AS reversed_data FROM test_table a JOIN test_table b ON a.id = b.id - 1 WHERE a.data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT reverse(data) AS reversed_data, COUNT(*) AS count FROM test_table WHERE created_at - INTERVAL 1 DAY > TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 00:00:00' GROUP BY reversed_data HAVING count > 1;
SELECT reverse(data || ' - ' || CAST(created_at AS VARCHAR)) AS complex_reverse FROM test_table WHERE data IS NOT NULL;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, created_at) VALUES (4, '', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-04 00:00:00');
SELECT reverse(NULLIF(data, '')) AS reversed_data FROM test_table;
SELECT reverse(data), created_at FROM test_table WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL ORDER BY created_at - INTERVAL 5 DAY;
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT reverse(b.data) FROM temp_table a LEFT JOIN test_table b ON a.id = b.id + 1 WHERE b.data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (created_at - INTERVAL 5 DAY))) AS average_epoch FROM test_table WHERE data IS NOT NULL;
